title: Fix GpuParticlesAttractorVectorField3D not updating preexisting GpuParticles3D
body: Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/113833 Previously GpuParticlesAttractorFieldVector3D would only apply correctly to GpuParticles3D that were created on the same frame or later than the GpuParticlesAttractorVectorField3D. See issue for more details and a minimal reproduction. It looks like the intention of the logic here was to update the textures for the particles when they are first created (`uniform_set_valid == false`), or when the set of textures overlapping with the particles has changed (`different == true`). However, it seems like the logic for the latter case was incomplete, and that path was never being taken. I confirmed that the minimal reproduction is fixed by these changes.

title: Fix Control minimum size check too aggressive
body: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116289 CodeTextEditor changes the RichTextLabel's minimum size based on its content height when it resizes. This causes it to resize again with a different content height and it continues going back and forth between 2 values. RichTextLabel can have a different content height even if only the minimum size height changes, because the scrollbar may show or not depending on if it fits. There is also no reserve scrollbar mode like ScrollContainer has. The error RichTextLabel changes size and causes its minimum height to be set which calls `Control::update_minimum_size()` then `Control::_update_minimum_size()`, which causes the size to change again. But this time the HBoxContainer it is in gets resorted before `Control::_update_minimum_size()` is called since it is deferred. The HBoxContainer resort updates the size which sets the minimum height again back to the original value. Previously (before 115596) it would then just be ignored since it has the same value as before, even though it changed and its effects got overridden. So in some ways it would be correct to update the size again in this case, but it also allows infinite loops to happen. In the future, RichTextLabel should probably be able to reserve the scrollbar space or do something for when it is at minimum size, since it isn't great at either of the values it is trying to use. At the lower height (26) without a scrollbar, you can see the `E r r` text go outside of its rect (4.6, this PR): And at the higher height (62) with a scrollbar, the scrollbar cannot move and the text is on one line (master, increasing size causes the freeze): It looks like there are also cases where the change in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/115596 updates the size where it isn't necessary, its too aggressive in updating sizes. So I am reverting that change and implementing a different fix for https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/109497 that only affects Controls that are made invisible but also doesn't resize them when invisible.

title: AnimationMixer: Fix track cache hash collisions
body: Summary Fixes AnimationMixer track cache collisions where different animation tracks with the same hash can alias to the same cache entry and animate the wrong target. Problem When two track paths collide on hash, AnimationMixer may reuse the wrong TrackCache. This can cause incorrect blending/application (e.g. one track influencing another property/node), as reported in the issue repro. Root cause Track cache lookup and reuse relied on hash identity too heavily, so hash collisions were not fully disambiguated at cache resolution/use sites. Solution Updated animation_mixer.cpp and animation_mixer.h to: resolve track cache entries by (thash + path + cache type) instead of hash alone, while keeping hash as the primary index; use collision probing to find/store a unique cache key when hashes conflict; make per-track weight deduplication key off TrackCache * identity rather than hash value; keep the existing cache architecture with minimal, targeted changes. Testing Built editor locally on Windows with incremental SCons build. Validated the collision scenario from the issue (colliding track paths) and confirmed tracks are resolved independently without cross-influence. Checked normal animation playback behavior to ensure no obvious regressions. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/99c6052d-d88f-441c-9886-d6febf5fc5c1 Notes Minimal, targeted fix for hash-collision handling in AnimationMixer cache lookup/reuse. No scripting API changes. Fixes 116231

title: Circumvent `Projection`'s getters inconsistencies with GPU transformed matrices
body: Circumvents inconsistent behavior of the `Projection`'s getters for z-near, z-far, aspect ratio, half-extents and pixel-per-meters, when called on GPU projection matrices, as per @clayjohn's suggestion in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108250issuecomment-3037134957 : > I am unhappy that we have exposed the transformed projection directly, we should have exposed it as an array of floats or something to make it clear that it is just for passing to the GPU. - Add `RenderSceneData::get_transformed_projection_data()` that returns the transformed projection matrix as a `PackedFloat32Array` to avoid `Projection`'s getters be called directly. - Add `RenderSceneData::get_z_near()`, `get_z_far()`, `get_aspect()`, `get_viewport_half_extents()`, `get_far_plane_half_extents()` and `get_pixels_per_meter()`. These getters either return member variables of `RenderSceneData` when available or wrap calls to the non-transformed `Projection` object. - Deprecate `RenderSceneData::get_cam_projection()`. It could also be left as is and just issue a `WARN_PRINT_ONCE`. I'm leaving this open for discussion below. - Update class documentation accordingly. Closes 106312 Supersedes 108250 Supersedes 106314

title: Capture XR to Texture for grabbing full eye seamlessly using compositor
body: As discussed in the XR channel, we had issues on our version of the engine capturing the viewport. This was required for a feedback system in Augmental Puzzles. Our requirements were: Capture the full eye Minimal overhead Avoid any frame hitch on XR We implemented this purely as a Vulkan capture similar to how the screen blit is done, though in this PR I have expanded it to include GLES so I could move it into the base compositor. I have not tested the GLES side as I'm less familiar with that part of the code, so it could definitely use a review by someone more familiar with that side. I've sketched out rough docs and a demo. Demo project using the feature: blit-capture-demo.zip AI Disclosure: Claude Opus was used to review this code and help me with the GLES conversion (as I'm less familiar with Godot's GLES implementation), though I have reviewed all the code and there is no direct slop in here. I also used it to write a first draft and spell-check my edits on the docs.

title: Theme variations are ignored when getting theme properties of a type other than the caller's class
body: Tested versions 4.7.dev [40448082abe972daa179c64d7889cd88a8e7ea9c]; also 4.4.1 System information Windows 11, Godot 4.7.dev, Issue description I got an issue with theme type variations, maybe it's due to a lack of understanding: When some parts of a Control node are drawn, theme items are obtained with no particular `theme_type`, in which case variations are applied normally. Sometimes however, a type is specified which does not match the class_name of the drawing node. As an example, I am working on a Godot plugin, and wanted to use the `EditorSpinSlider`, and I'd like to style its font color. The `EditorSpinSlider` which extends `Range` draws the value with a font color, that it obtains via `get_theme_color("font_color", "LineEdit")`. Since the `EditorSpinSlider` is not a `LineEdit`, it is not possible to provide a theme variation that affects the color of the label, regardless of whether the base type of the variation is Node, LineEdit or EditorSpinSlider. The label however, which gets its font color with a call to `get_theme_color("label_color")` can be styled using a variation. This affects a lot of editor nodes, but not exclusively. Another example would be the `"scroll_speed"` constant on a scroll container, or the `"save"`, `"load"` and `"clear"` icons in a color picker (shown when you open the three-dot menu on the swatches), which are obtained from within the ColorPicker class, but cannot be changed with a variation on it. I did notice that the icons can be changed, by e.g. adding an icon item `"save"` in the `FileDialog` type to the theme, but there is no way to style those using variations. Is this a bug, or what is the intention behind disallowing variations to affect certain parts of a node? I asked this in the contributor chat, but got no answer. In essence, in `theme_owners.cpp` -> `void ThemeOwner::get_theme_type_dependencies()`, why the restriction to `p_theme_type == type_variation`? Steps to reproduce Create a Theme and add it to a ColorPicker node. Add a Theme Variation `MyColorPicker`, with `ColorPicker` base, and add a `save` icon item. Run scene, click on the swatches three-dot, and observe that the `save` icon is ignored. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) theme-variation-issue.zip

title: Animation: Fix timeline length control clipping
body: Summary Fixes clipping of the Animation Timeline right-side length controls under large editor fonts/scales. Problem When editor font/scale is increased, the timeline length controls can be partially clipped, making them hard to read/edit. The issue is reproducible when main_font_size is around 20+ (see issue details). Root cause Timeline minimum sizing did not fully account for: actual width needed by the length text field in current theme font, and full vertical size requirements of timeline handle and right-side controls. Solution Updated animation_track_editor.cpp to: apply a font-aware minimum width for the length field on theme changes, and include label/timeline-handle/right-controls heights in timeline minimum size calculation. Testing Built editor locally on Windows with incremental SCons build. Opened AnimationPlayer timeline and verified no clipping with larger font sizes/scales (including main_font_size around 20+). Confirmed no obvious regressions at normal scale/font settings. Notes Minimal, targeted UI sizing fix. No API changes. Fixes 115838

title: Editor hangs after changing font parameters
body: Tested versions Reproducible in: 4.6 RC 1, 4.7.dev1, v4.6.dev5.official [f5918a9d3], v4.6.dev4.official [bd2ca13c6] Not reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1], v4.6.dev3.official [9d84f3d13] Seems to have started in v4.6.dev4.official [bd2ca13c6] System information Godot v4.7.dev1 - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9144) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12600K (16 threads) - 31.74 GiB memory Issue description I have a test scene with 2000 labels. I'm using a default theme called main_theme. The default font is a FontVariation. When I make changes to my font's parameters (extra spacing, baseline offset, etc) I can see the changes in real-time when I drag to adjust the parameters, but once I release my mouse button or stay still too long, Godot hangs for a while. It's seems to be affected by how much I change the parameters. If I adjust the parameters too much, it can make the editor hang for almost a minute. Also, after this, switching scenes continue to take about as long as the last hang. However, if I save and reopen the project, it goes back to normal. In all the versions before 4.6.dev4 I've tested, I'm able to immediately continue editing, panning the camera around, itc, but from 4.6.dev4 onwards it hangs. CPU and GPU usage never goes above 5%. While testing, I've noticed the problem compounds. The more I mess with the FontVariation, the longer and longer it'll freeze. It's up to well over a minute now. The font I'm using is Silver which supports lots of languages. I thought that might be contributing to the problem, but I've tested it with smaller fonts and it still happens. Steps to reproduce 1. Open the project 2. Double click the main_theme to open it in the inspector 3. Mouse drag on one of the parameters (i.e. Extra spacing: Bottom) 4. The changes update immediately 5. Release your mouse (pausing too long while dragging can trigger it too) 6. Godot will hang 7. Switch between scene_1 and scene_2 8. Godot will hang 9. Reload the project 10. Switch between scene_1 and scene_2 11. Godot will act normal Minimal reproduction project (MRP) MRP.zip

title: Provide default values for fold state in "Edit Animation Libraries" window to prevent errors
body: Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116293. The "Edit Animation Libraries" window would try to get folding data even if the file with that data had not been created yet. Because no default value was provided, the error described in 116293 would be thrown. This PR provides empty PackedStringArrays as default values for these `get_value()` calls, so that if there is no folding data, the editor considers no sections to be folded.

title: Fix mouse cursor shape change interrupting input events
body: Fixes 116253 Description: Changing mouse_default_cursor_shape inside gui_input was causing an immediate call to update_mouse_cursor_state(). This immediate update interrupted the event propagation flow, preventing subsequent signals like pressed from being emitted. Changes: Modified Control::set_default_cursor_shape to use call_deferred for the cursor state update. This ensures the cursor change happens at the end of the frame, allowing the current input event to complete its life cycle naturally. Verification: Tested with the MRP provided in the issue. Before the fix, the pressed signal was never triggered. After the fix, the signal fires correctly for every click while the cursor still updates as expected.

title: Fix animation player crash when setting current animation to stop
body: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115105 - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/98076 - Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/115126 - Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98704 The fundamental issue is that calling `seek()` inside `stop()` to reset the state causes `set_current_animation()` to be called recursively. The following changes will prevent the crash: 1. Make `stop()` a deferred call 2. Limit the case setting `current_animation` and triggering the `stop()` only when `is_playing()` is `true` Also, since stopping via `current_animation` clears the `AnimationPlayer`'s `current_animation`, implement a fallback in `AnimationPlayerEditor` for when `current_animation` is empty. And remove unused flag and comment.

title: Fix signal-related crash
body: Related to: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/94666 While we're also not able to track down the exact root cause of the crash, nor produce an MRP, we have seen it in the wild. The dump file produced shows that `c.signal.get_object()` returns `NULL`, and then when `disconnect` is called on it, the crash occurs. The code looks obviously wrong - it checks for the existence of one object, then proceeds to call a function on another. ------- Although we didn't find a root cause, here is a little more information on our investigation, in case it helps someone in the future. Here is the stack trace of our crash: And here is the threads view: Interestingly, the crash is taking place on a .NET finalizer thread, meaning it occurs when a .NET wrapper object is disposing of a Godot `Object`. That leads me to believe there is a subtle race condition somewhere, but there's not much more information on what that would be.

title: Localization bug: Typo in Russian translation of 3D Viewport "View Information" stats (uses 'ф' instead of 'f')
body: Tested versions godot 4.6.1 rc1 System information Godot v4.6.1.rc1 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated GeForce 920M - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-5005U CPU @ 2.00GHz (4 threads) - 11.91 ГиБ memory Issue description In the Russian localization of Godot 4.6.1, there is a critical typo in the string used for displaying 3D Viewport statistics. A Cyrillic letter "ф" is used instead of the technical character "f" in a format specifier. This causes the debugger to spam a "Formatting error" 60 times per second whenever the 3D scene information overlay is active. Steps to reproduce Set the editor language to Russian (Interface -> Editor -> Language -> Russian). Open any 3D scene. In the 3D model viewport, open the "Perspective" menu. Turn on "View Info." Open the "Output" bottom panel. Observed behavior: The console is filled with the following error: ERROR: Formatting error in string "Размер: %s (%.1фМП)": unsupported format character. Technical Root Cause: The error is caused by the string Размер: %s (%.1фМП). The engine expects %.1f (float format) to display memory usage, but because it's written with a Russian 'ф', the formatting engine fails to recognize the command and throws an error. Expected behavior: The text should display the memory/geometry size correctly (e.g., "1.5 MB") without any errors in the console. Workaround: Switching the editor to English or disabling "View Information" stops the error. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: Fix type mismatch in ADD_PROPERTY calls
body: ADD_PROPERTY call requires a type to be specified. But during document generation, the member's type is inferred from the getter's return type. The first patch adds an error log to indicate mismatch during document generation. The second patch fixes the issues detected in the current code.

title: Wayland: Only handle the current output mode
body: Fixes 115369. Some compositors (e.g. COSMIC) can report *all* supported output modes, not just the current mode. This is valid, albeit deprecated, so let's add a check and ignore any non-current output events. --- This meant that, on certain compositors, `screen_get_size` could return the wrong size, even really tiny stuff like 640x480. That said, even with this fix, the way we handle screens is still a hack and we should not depend on them in the long term.
